observations.summary: The board agreed to proceed with initial steps to explore strategic alternatives, including a potential sale of the Company.
The board decided to engage in a process to consider strategic alternatives.
The board engaged BofA Merrill Lynch as financial advisor for the strategic alternatives review.
New Mountain Capital executed a confidentiality agreement with the Company.
Five unnamed bidders, comprising four financial buyers and one strategic buyer, submitted preliminary non-binding indications of interest ranging from $20.00 to $22.00 per share.
Party X contacted BofA Merrill Lynch on an unsolicited basis to inquire about the process.
Party Y contacted BofA Merrill Lynch on an unsolicited basis to inquire about the process.
Party X submitted a preliminary non-binding indication of interest ranging from $21.50 to $23.00 per share.
Party X informed BofA Merrill Lynch that it was no longer interested in pursuing a potential transaction with the Company.
observations.other_detail: Party X informed representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch that it was no longer interested in pursuing a potential transaction with the Company.

observations.summary: Party Y submitted a preliminary non-binding indication of interest ranging from $19.50 to $20.50 per share.
The board terminated the process to explore strategic alternatives.
observations.other_detail: The board ended the 2014 sale process because of limited buyer interest and uncertainty from the Marietta aerosol-facility fire.

observations.summary: New Mountain Capital met with BofA Merrill Lynch and expressed interest in discussions with the Company regarding a potential transaction.
New Mountain Capital delivered an unsolicited indication of interest to acquire the Company for $19.25 per share.
The board resumed sale negotiations with New Mountain Capital after the prior process had been terminated.
observations.other_detail: After the June 26, 2014 termination, the board reopened the process by moving forward with negotiations with New Mountain Capital.

observations.summary: New Mountain Capital delivered a revised indication of interest at $20.05 per share and said that was its highest price.
New Mountain Capital stated that its $20.05 per share price was its best and final offer.

date.raw_text: Following the meeting of our board of directors

observations.summary: The parties executed the merger agreement and related transaction documents following the April 7 board meeting.

date.raw_text: Following the meeting of our board of directors

observations.summary: The parties executed the merger agreement and related transaction documents following the April 7 board meeting.
The parties issued a press release announcing the transaction on the morning of April 8, 2015.

exclusions.explanation: June 20, 2013 reflects the board's first discussion of strategic alternatives; the board did not authorize initial steps until July 1, 2013.
August 20, 2013 reflects the board deciding it was not an appropriate time to explore strategic alternatives, not a terminated sale process.
The June 2014 waiver-letter discussion summarizes the aftermath of the terminated 2014 process rather than a new bidder, restart, or proposal event.
New Mountain Capital's early-April reiteration of $20.05 per share repeated the March 13 best-and-final position without changed economics.
